summarization.a: The diagram illustrates a chamber setup with nitrogen, water, and TMA entering the chamber, and emissions exiting

a.question: Where are surface deposition reactions enabled in this numerical model?
a.answer: Only on the chamber bottom wall, where the wafer substrates are placed.

a.question: What does the textured green surface in this figure represent?
a.answer: The computational mesh used for finite volume method (FVM) discretization in the CFD simulation.

a.question: How many gas inlets versus outlets does this reactor geometry have?
a.answer: Three inlets (nitrogen, TMA, water) and one outlet (emissions).

a.question: Why is the computational domain simplified to enable reactions only on the bottom wall?
a.answer: In actual ALD processes, materials deposit on all inner surfaces of the system. However, for computational simplicity and efficiency, the numerical model restricts deposition reactions to the chamber bottom where wafers are placed. This allows focused study of film growth on substrates without the computational cost of tracking reactions on all surfaces.
